visualizations.title: Mathematical Definition of a Factor
visualizations.description: If two integers multiply to equal a third, both are factors of that third integer.

spec.main_latex: a \times b = c \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad a \text{ and } b \text{ are factors of } c

steps.explanation: Divisibility: This means a divides c completely, leaving no remainder.
Range: Positive factors are always greater than or equal to 1, and less than or equal to the number itself.

visualizations.title: Rectangular Grid Layouts of 12
visualizations.description: Watch how 12 dots can be arranged into different rectangular grids, illustrating the factor pairs: (1, 12), (2, 6), and (3, 4).
